subject: Your personalized electronic table of contents has arrived
body:
F1000Prime | http://f1000.com/prime 
=====================================
 
OCTOBER 2013

As you are a subscriber to the F1000 Monthly Update, we are writing to let you know that F1000Prime [ http://f1000.com/prime ] has evolved. We've introduced a new personalized and customizable homepage for all users of F1000Prime. The new F1000Prime is, for a limited time, freely available for a one-month trial to users who register or sign in online.

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 Try it now || http://f1000.com/prime/
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

In three simple steps you can create a highly personalized feed of articles recommended in F1000Prime, and new articles in PubMed, based on your own keywords and research interests. You just need to choose some keywords, confirm if our new self-learning algorithm found the right subject areas and Faculty Members [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty ] for you, and F1000Prime does the rest.

The system continues to learn and improve through your interactions with it -- irrelevant articles can be removed, and you can follow articles [ http://blog.f1000.com/2013/04/08/follow-articles-and-faculty-members/ ] you are particularly interested in. Read more about the new homepage's features on our blog [ http://blog.f1000.com/2013/10/22/new-prime-homepage/ ].

With these exciting developments to our service, now is an excellent time to come back to F1000Prime. Please register or sign in to F1000Prime [ http://f1000.com/prime/register ] to try out these new features for free for one month (no payment details are needed).

If you want to continue accessing F1000Prime immediately please subscribe online, or recommend F1000Prime to your organization's library with this form [ http://f1000.com/prime/recommend/academic ].

Kind regards,
Iain

Iain Hrynaszkiewicz
Outreach Director, F1000Prime

=====================================

You have received this message in the belief that it would be of interest.
To stop receiving this type of information from Faculty of 1000, reply to this email with the word "remove" in the subject line. For further information or enquiries please contact info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

F1000, Faculty of 1000 Ltd | Science Navigation Group          
Registered in England and Wales with Company Number 3739756.        
Middlesex House | 34-42 Cleveland Street | London W1T 4LB | United Kingdom
body_html:
To ensure you receive F1000 emails, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist. View web version.
OCTOBER 2013
As you are a subscriber to the F1000 Monthly Update, I'm writing to let you know that F1000Prime has evolved. We've introduced a new personalized and customizable homepage for all users of F1000Prime. A free one month trial personal subscription of the new F1000Prime is, for a limited time, available to new and returning users who register or sign in online.

In three simple steps you can create a highly personalized feed of articles recommended in F1000Prime, and new articles in PubMed, based on your own keywords and research interests. You just need to choose some keywords, confirm if our new self-learning algorithm found the right subject areas and Faculty Members for you, and F1000Prime does the rest.

The system continues to learn and improve through your interactions with it -- irrelevant articles can be removed, and you can follow articles you are particularly interested in. Read more about the new homepage's features on our blog.


With these exciting developments to our service, now is an excellent time to come back to F1000Prime. Please register or sign in to F1000Prime to try out these new features for free for one month (no payment details are needed).

If you want to continue accessing F1000Prime immediately please subscribe online, or recommend F1000Prime to your organization's library with this form.

Kind regards,
Iain

Iain Hrynaszkiewicz
Outreach Director, F1000Prime
